COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendant Benjamin P.D. Cannon.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:17:17 a.m., pausing at 11:22:55 a.m., reconvening at 11:27:26 a.m. and concluding at 11:35:44 a.m.

Plaintiff Andrew G. Watters appearing in pro per.
Defendant Benjamin P.D. Cannon not appearing.

Notice of Time and Place of Trial and Trial Order issued and mailed to Plaintiff and Defendant's Counsel on Aug-08-2022.

Defendant's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel filed on Jan-18-2023 and scheduled for hearing on Mar-21-2023 in Department 501.
Substitution of Attorney filed for Defendant on Feb-23-2023.

Court cannot determine the status of legal representation for Defendant or confirm Defendant received notice of trial. Court intends to continue this matter for proper notice to be sent to Defendant, individually and it's counsel of record.

Plaintiff presents oral argument in support of its objection to a continuance in this matter.

The matter is submitted.
Court orders matter continued to Mar-22-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for commencement of short cause trial.

Based on the register of actions of the case and comments made by Plaintiff regarding Defendant's attempts to evade service, the clerk shall serve notice of hearing on Defendant indivually, Defendant's counsel of record, and Plaintiff. Plaintiff shall serve Defendant with any filings pertaining to this action.

Holly Matin, SBN #322398; 818-913-9763; appearing on behalf of moving party, Alex Verbeck.

DEFENDANT LADY BENJAMIN CANNON'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is continued to March 21, 2023 at 9:30am in Department 501 for the moving party to file a proof of service and deliver courtesy copies to Department 501. Counsel to give notice of new hearing date.
